svg.text: NID API — build phases, team, and cost
Audit existing NID DB schema (field types,
completeness, duplicates, encoding)
Define canonical data model for API output
Draft consent framework & legal basis
Security pen-test of existing NID infra
Team: 2 senior engineers + 1 legal
Tech: PostgreSQL audit, data profiling tools
Risk: NID DB may have inconsistent encoding,
missing fields for ~40% of records
Cost: NPR 8–12M (3 months, small team)
Build OAuth 2.0 / PKCE auth server
3 endpoints: /verify, /kyc_basic, /otp
Rate limiting: 1000 req/min per app key
Developer portal + sandbox with fake NIDs
Audit log: every query logged with purpose
Team: 6 engineers, 1 PM, 1 security lead
Stack: Go/Node.js API, PostgreSQL audit DB,
Redis rate limiter, Keycloak for OAuth
Deploy: on-prem NTC DC + cloud backup
Cost: NPR 35–50M (6 months, 8-person team)
Citizen consent portal (web + mobile)
Granular scope approval UI per app
Add /kyc_photo endpoint (face match)
Citizen data access log ("who queried me")
Onboard first 10 pilot apps (banks, fintech)
Team: same core + 2 mobile devs + UX
Biometric: integrate with existing NID photo
store — no new hardware needed initially
Risk: photo quality of existing NID records
Cost: NPR 25–35M (6 months)
Public API documentation (OpenAPI 3.0 spec)
App registration portal for developers
Tiered access: sandbox free, prod paid/approved
SLA: 99.9% uptime, <200ms p95 latency
GDPR-equivalent data protection regulation
Full ecosystem opens — any registered company
can build KYC, login, or verification features
This is the Aadhaar moment for Nepal fintech
Total cost all phases: NPR 80–110M
~18 months to full production ecosystem
what to build
team / tech / cost
